com.mentor.chs.plugin.task

Interface IXTaskContext


  • @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXTaskContext
    IXTaskContext provides access to all the relevant information of a custom task under execution.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXTaskOutputAttachment createTaskAttachment(java.lang.String name)
      This is the API called by custom task implementation to create brand new attachments.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXTaskInputAttachment> getInputTaskAttachments()
      The set of input attachments pertaining to this task.
      java.lang.String getTaskExecutionID()
      Get unique ID of the task execution.
      java.lang.String getTaskID()
      Get unique ID of the task.

    • Method Detail

      • getTaskID

        java.lang.String getTaskID()
        Get unique ID of the task.
        Returns:
        unique identifier of the task

      • getTaskExecutionID

        java.lang.String getTaskExecutionID()
        Get unique ID of the task execution. When the task is repeated this ID changes, but task ID remains the same
        Returns:
        unique identifier of the task execution

      • getInputTaskAttachments

        java.util.Set<? extends IXTaskInputAttachment> getInputTaskAttachments()
        The set of input attachments pertaining to this task. For more details on the contents of the attachment, please refer to IXTaskInputAttachment
        Returns:
        attachments

      • createTaskAttachment

        IXTaskOutputAttachment createTaskAttachment(java.lang.String name)
        This is the API called by custom task implementation to create brand new attachments. For more details on the contents of the attachment, please refer to IXTaskOutputAttachment
        Parameters:
        name - attachment name. Attachments created by custom task shouldn’t have name field starting with the “capital_” as it is reserved for the capital use.
        Returns:
        output attachment. Plugin can write to this output and it will be persisted only at the end of task execution
